news.title:
India Ratings and CRISIL affirm SBI's rating at IND AAA Stable, highlighting market dominance, strong capitalization, and asset quality.
Tier I bonds rated IND AA Stable due to risk features; Tier II bonds rated IND AAA Stable.
news.summary:
India Ratings and CRISIL affirm SBI's rating at IND AAA Stable, highlighting market dominance, strong capitalization, and asset quality.
Tier I bonds rated IND AA Stable due to risk features; Tier II bonds rated IND AAA Stable.

news.title:
₹6,000 Cr frauds in IDFC First Bank, PNB, SBI raise concerns over governance and risk controls.

news.title:
NSE has initiated its IPO process, inviting pitches from 20 i-banks and 10 law firms, with Rothschild as an independent advisor.
NSE reported FY25 revenue of Rs 19,177 crore (up 17%) and PAT of Rs 12,188 crore (up 47%), highlighting strong growth.
news.summary:

news.title:
IT stocks lead recovery post Anthropic-triggered selloff; Nifty reclaims 25,500, Sensex gains 485.2 points to trade at 82,711.1.

news.title:
Nifty IT Index slid 20% in a year, while BFSI grew strongly, reshaping corporate profit mix and supporting broader market.
Large-cap IT stocks like TCS, Infosys fell 20%, while SBI surged 25%, with PSU banks gaining 50% in a year amid robust credit growth.
news.summary:
Nifty IT Index slid 20% in a year, while BFSI grew strongly, reshaping corporate profit mix and supporting broader market.
Large-cap IT stocks like TCS, Infosys fell 20%, while SBI surged 25%, with PSU banks gaining 50% in a year amid robust credit growth.

news.title:
NPS Tier II schemes offer flexible investments with no lock-in, delivering up to 18.8% in equity, 8.6% in corporate bonds, and 9.1% in government bonds.
Top performers include Tata Pension Fund (18.8% equity), HDFC Pension Fund (8.6% corporate bonds), and LIC (9.1% government bonds).
news.summary:
NPS Tier II schemes offer flexible investments with no lock-in, delivering up to 18.8% in equity, 8.6% in corporate bonds, and 9.1% in government bonds.
Top performers include Tata Pension Fund (18.8% equity), HDFC Pension Fund (8.6% corporate bonds), and LIC (9.1% government bonds).
